reachability_artifacts: cli-run: go test -count=1 -v -run TestRunSlice_CostSourceThreadedToStatusJSON ./internal/run/ — drives the real RunSlice engine entry point (Rule 1: not a leaf state.Dispatch struct literal), asserts the WRITTEN status.json's verification.dispatches[].cost_source carries two distinct driver-reported CostSource values through unmodified, and that the file still validates against slice-status-v1 — see proof.json reachability.evidence

internal/driver/claude_test.go:TestClaudeEnvelopeExplicitZeroCostIsUnknown (D1: a real fake-CLI subprocess spawn with an envelope carrying an explicit reported zero classifies CostSourceUnknown, not CostSourceSubscription)

internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess_test.go:TestInprocessImplementerPricingTable (AC-02: InProcess.Dispatch against a real httptest server with a priced model ID computes CostUSD from the true accumulated token split via the pricing registry, CostSource=pricing-table)
internal/driver/inprocess/inprocess_test.go:TestInprocessImplementer (AC-04: an unpriced confirmed model-id records CostUSD=0/CostSource=unknown through the same Dispatch path)
internal/model/pricing_test.go:TestPricingUnified (AC-01/R-01: every model key across all four provider pricing maps resolves through PriceForModel to the exact source-map rate)

internal/driver/driver_test.go:TestCostSourceVocabulary (re-entry session, closes verifier round-1 Gate 3: pins all five CostSource* constants' persisted string values, including CostSourceProviderReported=="provider")

effort_complexity.rationale: Mechanical but wide: two pricing tables unified, two flat-rate functions deleted, cost_source threaded driver->Result->Dispatch->status.json.

design_decisions.choice: D1 — claude.go costSource() ships CostSourceUnknown for BOTH a nil and an explicit-zero TotalCostUSD; only a strictly positive TotalCostUSD earns CostSourceCLI. Design.md's own proposal (TotalCostUSD==0 -> CostSourceSubscription) is NOT implemented.
options: Infer subscription from an explicit reported zero (TotalCostUSD != nil && == 0) — matches documented `claude -p` behaviour when authenticated against a Claude subscription rather than an API key (design.md's own proposal, flagged for ratification).

Ship every case as "unknown" until the inference is verified against a live subscription-authenticated CLI run (chosen) — no positively identified, testable marker in current CLI output distinguishes 'explicit zero because subscription' from 'explicit zero because a genuinely free/no-cost turn or a protocol quirk'; claudeEnvelope carries no field that disambiguates the two.
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach) ratified fail-closed: ship "unknown" for both the nil and the explicit-zero case — an explicit reported zero is NOT, by itself, a positively identified subscription marker. Do NOT implement the TotalCostUSD==0 -> "subscription" inference; only a non-zero TotalCostUSD (CostSource="cli") is positively identified. Record the absent marker as a Rule 2 note in proof.json. (captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 1, restated verbatim in this implementer session's task brief.)
design_decisions.rationale: A fabricated or guessed dollar figure — or an under-confident "subscription" label that hides real API spend — is precisely the dishonesty sworn#70 (this slice's own rationale) exists to close. An honest "unknown" is worth more to the telemetry data-moat than a plausible guess that poisons it.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — codex.go costSource() always returns CostSourceUnknown; the Usage!=nil -> CostSourceSubscription inference design.md proposed is NOT implemented.
options: Infer subscription from Usage != nil (a turn actually completed) on the theory that codex CLI is subscription-authenticated by default and its JSONL stream never reports cost by design (design.md's own proposal, flagged for ratification).

Ship every case as "unknown" until verified against a live codex binary (chosen) — codex.go's own header already flags its envelope shape as unverified (SIT-deferred); Usage != nil proves a turn happened, not that it was subscription-covered rather than API-billed; codexEnvelope carries no field distinguishing the two.
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad (Coach) ratified fail-closed: ship "unknown" universally for codex.go in this slice. Do NOT implement the Usage!=nil -> "subscription" inference. Record the absent marker as a Rule 2 note in proof.json. (captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 1, restated verbatim in this implementer session's task brief.)
design_decisions.rationale: Same financial-honesty stake as D1 — codex CLI never reports cost in any documented shape, so Usage!=nil is evidence of activity, not of billing status.

design_decisions.choice: D3 — Named driver.CostSource* constants (mirroring the existing ErrKind* pattern) replace scattered CostSource string literals across producers and tests.
options: Named constants in internal/driver/driver.go, one per vocabulary value: provider, pricing-table, cli, subscription, unknown (chosen).
Continue with ad hoc string literals per call site — rejected: a typo ("pricing_table" vs "pricing-table") is schema-valid and fails silently since slice-status-v1 is additionalProperties:true.
design_decisions.human_decision: Confirmed as designed (memory-cited, pre-acknowledged captain-proceed.md@4753eb3 pin 5) — proceed with named constants.
design_decisions.rationale: Mirrors the ErrKindAuth-and-siblings precedent already binding on this track (project_provider_error_taxonomy, project_driver_contract_recut): a cross-cutting driver vocabulary gets named constants, not string literals.
